description: Labeled precision/recall corpus for the ScopeTrail permission-drift engine. Each case points at a fixture under benchmark/fixtures/ that holds an old/ and new/ snapshot of an AI-agent config tree; the runner diffs them with `scopetrail diff --old <case>/old --new <case>/new --format json` and scores the verdict against ground truth (intent, expected finding kinds, expected consolidated rating) derived from the fixture's design — not from re-running the tool. Benign cases are deliberate false-positive traps a naive textual diff would flag: tightened postures, removed servers, and reordered JSON keys.

cases.note: A new pinned MCP server appears in .mcp.json; addition alone is reportable.
New server launched via npx @vendor/...@latest — added and unpinned.
Same server name, pinned version bumped @1.0.0 -> @2.0.0.
New server points at an unencrypted http:// endpoint — remote endpoint is critical over plaintext.
New server points at an https:// endpoint — remote but encrypted, so high not critical.
Malformed new .mcp.json is reported, not silently dropped or crashed on.
A pinned server added to an example/template MCP config — informational, low.
Sample server installs via curl | bash; pipe-to-shell escalates the sample-unpinned finding to high.
Bare `Bash` verb added to the allowlist — broad, and bash escalates to high.
Bare `Read` verb added — broad but not bash/write/edit, so medium.
Read(.env) deny rule removed — secret-guarding deny removal is critical.
PreToolUse hook removed — a high-impact lifecycle hook.
PreToolUse hook command swapped to add --skip; high-impact hook.
Malformed new .claude/settings.json is reported, not hidden.
Codex sandbox widened to danger-full-access — top sandbox rank, critical.
Codex sandbox widened read-only -> workspace-write; elevation below full-access, so high.
Approval policy weakened to `never` — no human in the loop, high.
Codex network access newly enabled (false -> true).
A project entry newly marked trust_level = trusted.
New [mcp_servers.NAME] block added in Codex config, launched via npx -y @vendor/...@latest.
Malformed new .codex/config.toml is reported, not hidden.
One PR touching MCP, Claude, and Codex at once — five distinct drift classes.
old and new are byte-identical across all three surfaces; nothing should fire.
Every added allow is narrowly scoped (Read(src/...), WebFetch(domain:...), mcp__github__get_issue) and an added deny tightens — none is broad.
Every Codex setting moves to a tighter posture (full-access -> read-only, never -> on-request, network true -> false); drift detection ignores tightening.
network_access was already true in old; it is not newly enabled here, only an unrelated model line is added.

A brand-new .codex/config.toml pins the narrowest posture (read-only sandbox, untrusted approval) where the base had no Codex config; a missing baseline must anchor at Codex's safe default, not rank below every value, so the safest possible config is not reported as a widening/weakening.
A server is removed between old and new — removing scope is not drift; only additions are flagged.
Same launch command, JSON keys reordered — a semantic comparison must see no change where a textual diff would.
